title: Compliance Gap

scenario:
AEGIS TERMINAL v1.0 -- VERIDIAN SYSTEMS SOC

INCOMING TICKET -- COMPLIANCE TEAM
PRIORITY: HIGH
ANALYST: YOU

OPERATION IRONCLAD -- NIST CSF GAP ASSESSMENT

The CISO has requested a NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) compliance
assessment before the board presentation. You have been given 15 control
mappings across all 5 NIST CSF functions.

NIST CSF CONTROL STATUS:

  IDENTIFY:
    ID-AM-01  Asset inventory maintained              IMPLEMENTED
    ID-RA-01  Risk assessment conducted annually      IMPLEMENTED

  PROTECT:
    PR-AC-01  Identity management and access control  IMPLEMENTED
    PR-DS-01  Data-at-rest encryption                 IMPLEMENTED
    PR-IP-01  Configuration baseline documented       IMPLEMENTED

  DETECT:
    DE-AE-01  Network anomaly detection active        IMPLEMENTED
    DE-CM-01  Continuous monitoring in place          IMPLEMENTED

  RESPOND:
    RS-RP-01  Incident response plan documented       IMPLEMENTED
    RS-CO-01  Response team communications protocol   IMPLEMENTED

  RECOVER:
    RC-RP-01  Recovery plan documented                NOT IMPLEMENTED
    RC-RP-02  Business continuity plan tested         NOT IMPLEMENTED
    RC-IM-01  Recovery improvements tracked           NOT IMPLEMENTED
    RC-IM-02  Lessons learned formally captured       NOT IMPLEMENTED
    RC-CO-01  Recovery communications plan exists     NOT IMPLEMENTED
    RC-CO-02  External communications contacts list   IMPLEMENTED

Type 'tools' to run the compliance mapper and identify the biggest gap.

Which NIST CSF function has the lowest compliance percentage?
challenge: Which NIST CSF function has the lowest compliance percentage and represents the biggest gap?
hints:
Go to https://www.nist.gov/cyberframework
        Read about the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF).
        The 5 functions (Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover) form
        the backbone of cybersecurity program maturity assessment.

Calculate compliance per function:
        implemented controls / total controls for that function x 100
        Count how many controls are marked IMPLEMENTED vs NOT IMPLEMENTED
        for each of the 5 NIST CSF functions.
        The function with the lowest percentage is the biggest gap.
        Type 'tools' to compute the percentages.

Type 'tools' in the game. The compliance mapper calculates the percentage
        for each NIST CSF function and ranks them from lowest to highest.
        The function at the bottom of the list is your answer.

SPOILER -- RECOVER has only 1 of 6 controls implemented = 17% compliance.
        Identify, Protect, Detect, and Respond are all at 100%.
        The NIGHTWIRE incident exposed this gap -- Veridian had no documented
        recovery plan or business continuity test.
        Type: recover
tools: Maps implemented controls to NIST CSF functions (Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover), computes compliance percentage per function, and ranks functions from largest to smallest gap.
learn:
Compliance gap analysis identifies where an organization's security controls fall
short of a framework standard. The gap is the distance between current state and
target state.

NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) -- 5 Functions:

IDENTIFY (ID):
  Know your assets, risks, and business environment.
  Sub-categories: Asset Management (AM), Risk Assessment (RA), Risk Management (RM)
  Foundational -- you can't protect what you don't know you have.

PROTECT (PR):
  Implement safeguards to limit impact of threats.
  Sub-categories: Access Control (AC), Data Security (DS), Protective Technology (PT)

DETECT (DE):
  Identify cybersecurity events when they occur.
  Sub-categories: Anomalies (AE), Continuous Monitoring (CM), Detection Processes (DP)

RESPOND (RS):
  Take action on detected cybersecurity events.
  Sub-categories: Response Planning (RP), Communications (CO), Analysis (AN)

RECOVER (RC):
  Restore capabilities impaired by incidents.
  Sub-categories: Recovery Planning (RP), Improvements (IM), Communications (CO)

Gap analysis process:
  1. Map implemented controls to framework sub-categories
  2. Calculate compliance % per function (implemented / total x 100)
  3. Rank functions by compliance (lowest = biggest gap)
  4. Build remediation roadmap targeting lowest-compliance functions first
  5. Present to CISO/board with gap visualization (radar chart is common)

Common compliance frameworks on CySA+ exam:
  NIST CSF        -- voluntary, widely adopted, 5 functions
  NIST SP 800-53  -- federal systems, 20 control families
  ISO 27001       -- international, Annex A domains
  PCI-DSS         -- payment card industry, 12 requirements
  HIPAA           -- healthcare, safeguards (administrative, physical, technical)

steps.question: How many NIST CSF controls in the RECOVER function are listed as NOT IMPLEMENTED?
hints: Look only at the RECOVER function rows in the challenge_data or the scenario's control status table.
The RECOVER function has 6 controls: RC-RP-01, RC-RP-02, RC-IM-01, RC-IM-02, RC-CO-01, RC-CO-02.
RC-CO-02 is marked IMPLEMENTED. Count the remaining controls marked NOT IMPLEMENTED.
5 of the 6 RECOVER controls are NOT IMPLEMENTED: RC-RP-01, RC-RP-02, RC-IM-01, RC-IM-02, and RC-CO-01.

wrong_answer_hints.response: The RECOVER function has 6 total controls, but one (RC-CO-02) is implemented. Count only the NOT IMPLEMENTED ones.
Count again — RC-RP-01, RC-RP-02, RC-IM-01, RC-IM-02, and RC-CO-01 are all NOT IMPLEMENTED. That is 5.

steps.question: What is the compliance percentage for the RECOVER function (implemented controls / total controls × 100)?
hints: RECOVER has 6 total controls. Only 1 is implemented (RC-CO-02).
Compliance % = (implemented / total) × 100 = (1 / 6) × 100.
1 ÷ 6 = 0.1667. Multiply by 100 = 16.67%, which rounds to approximately 17%.
RECOVER compliance is 17% — the lowest of all 5 NIST CSF functions. All other functions are at 100%.

wrong_answer_hints.response: 83% would be 5 out of 6 — but that counts the NOT IMPLEMENTED controls. Only 1 of 6 is implemented, giving 17%.
Identify, Protect, Detect, and Respond are at 100%, but the question asks about RECOVER specifically.

steps.question: Which NIST CSF function has the lowest compliance percentage and represents the biggest gap?
hints: The 5 NIST CSF functions are: Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover. Calculate the compliance % for each.
Identify (2/2=100%), Protect (3/3=100%), Detect (2/2=100%), Respond (2/2=100%). Only one function has controls marked NOT IMPLEMENTED.
Type 'tools' to run the compliance mapper — it calculates and ranks all 5 functions by compliance percentage.
RECOVER has only 1 of 6 controls implemented = 17% compliance. All other functions are at 100%. RECOVER is the biggest gap.

wrong_answer_hints.response: RESPOND has 2 of 2 controls implemented = 100% compliance. Look at the RECOVER function instead.
IDENTIFY has 2 of 2 controls implemented = 100% compliance. The gap is in the RECOVER function.

steps.question: Which specific RECOVER sub-category has the most unimplemented controls — Recovery Planning (RP), Improvements (IM), or Communications (CO)?
hints: The RECOVER function has three sub-categories: RP (Recovery Planning), IM (Improvements), and CO (Communications).
RP controls: RC-RP-01 (not implemented), RC-RP-02 (not implemented) — 2 controls, both missing.
IM controls: RC-IM-01 (not implemented), RC-IM-02 (not implemented) — 2 controls, both missing. CO: RC-CO-01 (not implemented), RC-CO-02 (implemented) — 1 of 2 missing.
Both RP and IM have 0% compliance (2/2 controls missing each). CO has 50% compliance (1/2 implemented). RP and IM are tied for worst.

wrong_answer_hints.response: CO (Communications) has RC-CO-02 implemented, giving it 50% compliance. RP and IM are both at 0% — worse than CO.

debrief.summary: RECOVER has the lowest compliance at 17% (1 of 6 controls implemented). Identify, Protect, Detect, and Respond are all at 100%. The NIGHTWIRE incident directly exposed this gap -- Veridian had no documented recovery plan, no business continuity test, and no formal lessons-learned process. Operation IRONCLAD must prioritize RECOVER function controls.
debrief.real_world: NIST CSF compliance assessments are used by CISOs to build board-level security roadmaps. The gap analysis output (typically a radar/spider chart) visually shows which functions are mature vs. underdeveloped. After a major incident, the RECOVER function consistently shows the largest gaps. NIST CSF 2.0 (released 2024) added GOVERN as a sixth function.
debrief.next_step:
Explore the NIST CSF:
https://www.nist.gov/cyberframework
NIST CSF 2.0: https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/cswp/29/final
debrief.cert_link:
CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 4 -- Reporting and Communication:
"Given a scenario, perform compliance gap analysis and map controls to NIST CSF or other frameworks."
debrief.exam_tip: On the exam, compliance gap analysis questions test NIST CSF function knowledge. Know the 5 functions in order: Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover (IPDRR). Gap = lowest compliance percentage = biggest remediation priority. Know that ISO 27001 uses Annex A control domains (not functions), and PCI-DSS uses 12 requirements. NIST CSF 2.0 added GOVERN as a 6th function -- this may appear in newer exam versions.
